title: Ensure soft delete on Backup vaults is Enabled

input_row.title: Ensure soft delete on Backup vaults is Enabled
input_row.description: Soft delete provides additional protection for Backup vault data. With soft delete enabled, deleted backup data can be recovered within the retention period.
input_row.rationale: Important backup data could be accidentally deleted or removed by a malicious actor. With soft delete enabled, data is retained for at least 14 days before permanent deletion, allowing for the recovery of the backup data.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Backup vaults. 2. Click the name of a Backup vault. 3. Under Manage, click Properties. 4. Ensure Soft delete is set to Enabled or Enabled with Always-On. 5. Next to Soft delete, click Update. 6. Ensure that the Retention period is set to an appropriate value between 14 and 180, inclusive. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each Backup vault. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list Backup vaults: az dataprotection backup-vault list For each Backup vault, run the following command: az dataprotection backup-vault show --resource-group -- vault-name Ensure that under softDeleteSettings, state is set to on or alwayson, and retentionDurationInDays is set to an appropriate value between 14 and 180, inclusive. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 9798d31d-6028-4dee-8643-46102185c016 • Name: '[Preview]: Soft delete should be enabled for Backup Vaults'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Backup vaults. 2. Click the name of a Backup vault. 3. Under Manage, click Properties. 4. Next to Soft delete, click Update. 5. Check the box next to Soft delete. 6. In the box next to Retention period, set an appropriate number of days for deleted data to be retained, between 14 and 180, inclusive. 7. If it is appropriate for your organization, check the box next to Enable always- on soft delete. Note: Once enabled, this setting cannot be disabled. 8. Click Update. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each Backup vault requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI Run the following command to list Backup vaults: az dataprotection backup-vault list For each Backup vault requiring remediation, run the following command to enable soft delete and set an appropriate number of days for deleted data to be retained, between 14 and 180, inclusive: az dataprotection backup-vault update --resource-group -- vault-name --soft-delete-state On --retention-duration-in-days Note: To enable always-on soft delete, the above command can be executed with -- soft-delete-state AlwaysOn. Once enabled, this setting cannot be disabled.
input_row.impact: There is no additional cost for backup data in the soft delete state for up to and including 14 days. However, retention beyond 14 days may incur additional charges.
input_row.default_value: Soft delete is enabled by default on newly created Backup vaults.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit can be performed using AWS APIs, as similar functionality exists in AWS related to backup and recovery (for instance, AWS Backup). AWS provides APIs to interact with backup vaults and settings.
- The audit references the use of CLI commands which can be analogously utilized in AWS through the AWS CLI or Python's Boto3 SDK to check for similar configurations.
- There is a direct parallel between Azure's soft delete for backup vaults and AWS's backup solutions, which include settings for recovery management.
- The functionality can be queried using AWS APIs, which allows for automation of the verification process.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_backup_vault_soft_delete_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
- The automated program would check:
  - Utilize the AWS Backup APIs to enumerate backup vaults using `list_backup_vaults()`.
  - For each backup vault, use `describe_backup_vault(vault_name)` to retrieve details on whether soft delete is enabled and check the retention period.
  - Specifically, ensure that the soft delete setting is enabled (equivalent to Azure's "Soft delete is set to Enabled or Enabled with Always-On") and that the retention duration matches the required conditions (between 14 and 180 days).
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - If the soft delete status is "Enabled" or "Always-On", and the retention period falls within the stipulated range, the control would pass.
  - If it doesn't meet these criteria, the control would fail.
- This approach ensures compliance with the intended audit, facilitating an automated check on the configuration of soft delete settings in AWS Backup vaults comparable to Azure's Backup vaults.
